name: Navacerrada — circular by the Pinares de La Barranca route
name_original: Navacerrada — circular por la ruta Pinares de La Barranca
region_intro: Touring cycling around Navacerrada offers diverse routes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, characterized by its mountainous terrain. The region features extensive pine forests, significant mountain peaks like La Maliciosa, and the Navacerrada Reservoir. Cyclists can navigate a mix of valleys, ascents to mountain passes such as Puerto de Navacerrada, and scenic paths along the reservoir's banks. This landscape provides varied challenges and views for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Navacerrada The most popular touring cycling route is The Barranca and Cerro del Castillo by Navacerrada – circular from Becerril de la Sierra, a 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route leads through pine forests with varied ascents and offers views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Navacerrada — circular by the Pinares de La Barranca route, a difficult 15.7 miles (25.2 km) path. This route offers views of the Sierra de Guadarrama and passes through extensive pine forests. Local touring cyclists also love the Old Castle of Manzanares – San Isidro Hermitage loop from Becerril de la Sierra, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including pine forests and open landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Navacerrada is defined by pine forests, mountain passes, and the Navacerrada Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Navacerrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Navacerrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Navacerrada?
A: Touring cycling routes around Navacerrada offer a range of challenges. While there are 18 easy routes, the majority are moderate to difficult, with 45 moderate and 58 difficult routes available. This variety ensures options for different ability levels, from those seeking a relaxed ride to experienced cyclists looking for a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Navacerrada?
A: The terrain around Navacerrada is characterized by its mountainous nature within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You can expect a mix of valleys, ascents to mountain passes like Puerto de Navacerrada, and scenic paths through extensive pine forests. Routes often traverse varied landscapes, including open areas and dense woodlands.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Navacerrada?
A: Yes, Navacerrada offers 18 easy touring cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes often feature gentler gradients and less technical terrain, allowing you to enjoy the beautiful scenery without excessive challenge.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Navacerrada typically take to complete?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Navacerrada varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959822859/the-barranca-and-cerro-del-castillo-by-navacerrada-circular-from-becerril-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Barranca and Cerro del Castillo by Navacerrada – circular from Becerril de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30.2 km, typically takes around 2 hours 52 minutes. Longer or more challenging routes will naturally require more time.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Navacerrada?
A: While many routes in Navacerrada are moderate to difficult, the 18 easy routes are generally more suitable for families. These routes often follow less strenuous paths, such as those around the Navacerrada Reservoir, providing a pleasant experience for all ages. Always check the route details for specific difficulty and terrain before heading out with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Navacerrada?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, where Navacerrada is located, is a protected area with diverse wildlife. While dogs are generally permitted on trails, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local fauna and flora. Always check specific regulations for each route or area within the park to ensure a responsible and enjoyable experience for you and your dog.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Navacerrada?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Navacerrada are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189600613/navacerrada-circular-by-the-pinares-de-la-barranca-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada — circular by the Pinares de La Barranca route&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959822859/the-barranca-and-cerro-del-castillo-by-navacerrada-circular-from-becerril-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Barranca and Cerro del Castillo by Navacerrada – circular from Becerril de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;. These loops allow you to explore the region's beauty without retracing your steps.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Navacerrada's touring cycling routes offer access to stunning natural features and landmarks within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You can expect to see extensive pine forests, significant mountain peaks like La Maliciosa and La Bola del Mundo, and the picturesque Navacerrada Reservoir. Notable viewpoints include the Mirador de las Canchas, offering panoramic views. You might also encounter highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maliciosa Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4560049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Navacerrada?
A: The touring cycling experience in Navacerrada is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, including pine forests and mountain passes, and the spectacular views of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The variety of routes, from moderate to difficult, also contributes to its popularity among over 3000 visitors.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of La Pedriza – San Isidro Hermitage loop from Navacerrada&lt;/a&gt; are specifically highlighted for their scenic beauty. The Mirador de las Canchas, accessible via routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40019097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Village – Mirador de las Canchas loop from Navacerrada&lt;/a&gt;, provides outstanding panoramic views of the peaks and pine forests. The route around the Navacerrada Reservoir also offers spectacular natural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Navacerrada?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Navacerrada, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. While summer can be warm, the higher altitudes provide some relief. Winter cycling is possible, especially on lower-elevation routes, but be prepared for colder temperatures and potential snow at higher passes like Puerto de Navacerrada.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Navacerrada region?
A: There are over 120 touring cycling routes available around Navacerrada, offering a wide range of options for cyclists. This extensive network includes routes of varying lengths and difficulties, ensuring there's something for every preference and skill level.
tips: Beautiful dam, especially when it is up to the top of the water, in possibly one of the most beautiful villages in Spain. Beautiful views!

The Las Canchas viewpoint is an extraordinary panoramic point of the Barranca Valley, enclosed by the Guarramillas and Maliciosa massifs. The enigmatic and mysterious name of Maliciosa appears on several occasions in local chronicles from the fourteenth century and is usually related to tragic events that occurred to countrymen from nearby towns. The best known were the murders of the bandit Pablo Santos, in the Peña del Mediodía; of the shepherd nicknamed El Mirlo, on the Valdehalcones pass; and of another shepherd with an unknown name who died in the Peña de la Barranca.

Looking face to face at the mountain from the southern slope, it is easy to agree with the shepherds of yesteryear and “like Madaleta in the Pyrenees, Maliciosa is in Guadarrama, the cursed one; land of the most mistreated in the Sierra, where the rock, always desolate and naked, which time continuously sharpens and wears away, dies in its beauty, deeper and deeper”. The Pedriza del Real de Manzanares. Constancio Bernaldo de Quiros. Uneven Editions 1999.
